diseases.name: Hypertrophic Scarring
diseases.description: Excessive collagen deposition and fibroblast proliferation in wound healing, resulting in raised, thickened scars. TGF-beta/Smad signaling drives myofibroblast activation. Mechanotransduction via focal adhesion kinase perpetuates fibrosis.

diseases.name: Keloid Formation
diseases.description: Pathological scar tissue extending beyond original wound margins. Characterized by excessive extracellular matrix deposition, aberrant immune response, and sustained inflammation. Higher incidence in darker skin types suggests genetic predisposition.

diseases.name: Post-Surgical Adhesion
diseases.description: Abnormal fibrous bands forming between tissues and organs after surgery. Peritoneal adhesions cause bowel obstruction, chronic pain, and infertility. Mesothelial cell injury triggers fibrin deposition and incomplete fibrinolysis.

diseases.name: Dupuytren Contracture
diseases.description: Progressive fibroproliferative disease of palmar fascia causing finger contracture. Myofibroblasts in Dupuytren's nodules produce excess collagen. Wnt/beta-catenin signaling implicated. No effective non-surgical treatment exists.

diseases.name: Peyronie Disease
diseases.description: Fibrotic plaque formation in tunica albuginea of penis causing curvature, pain, and erectile dysfunction. TGF-beta1 drives fibroblast-to-myofibroblast transition. Oxidative stress and microvascular trauma initiate pathogenesis.
